title: KeyBank's New Check-in Tool Supports Small Business Growth and Financial Health
text: Interactive Tool Offers Direct Access to Financial Advice Between Small Business Owners and Bankers CLEVELAND , April 27, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Small businesses continue to face the considerable challenges of the pandemic, inflation, a labor shortage and an ever-changing economy. To succeed, having the proper resources, tools and advice at their disposal is a necessity.

title: KeyBank Announces Elimination of Non-Sufficient Fund Fees and Other Overdraft Changes
text: Changes will provide more support to help consumers manage their finances CLEVELAND , April 26, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- KeyBank (NYSE: KEY), a long-standing leader in helping clients move forward on their financial journey, announced the elimination of non-sufficient fund fees, a reduction in overdraft fees, and other steps to help clients manage their finances. Initially discussed at KeyCorp's Investor Day on March 1, 2022 , additional details are being released today.

title: KeyCorp (KEY) Down as Q1 Earnings Miss on Higher Provisions
text: Lower fee income and increased provisions hurt KeyCorp's (KEY) Q1 earnings. Robust loan demand resulting in higher NII and a marginal decline in costs are tailwinds.

title: KeyCorp (KEY) CEO Chris Gorman on Q1 2022 Results - Earnings Call Transcript
text: KeyCorp (NYSE:KEY ) Q1 2022 Earnings Conference Call April 21, 2022 8:00 AM ET Company Participants Chris Gorman - Chairman & Chief Executive Officer Don Kimble - Chief Financial Officer Mark Midkiff - Chief Risk Officer Conference Call Participants Peter Winter - Wedbush Securities Ebrahim Poonawala - Bank of America Steven Alexopoulos - JPMorgan John Pancari - Evercore ISI Gerard Cassidy - RBC Erika Najarian - UBS Mike Mayo - Wells Fargo Securities Betsy Graseck - Morgan Stanley Ken Usdin - Jefferies Matt O'Connor - Deutsche Bank Brian Foran - Autonomous Scott Siefers - Piper Sandler Operator Good morning, and welcome to KeyCorp's First Quarter 2022 Earnings Conference Call. As a reminder, this conference is being recorded.

title: KeyCorp (KEY) Q1 Earnings and Revenues Lag Estimates
text: KeyCorp (KEY) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of -6.25% and 3.74%, respectively, for the quarter ended March 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: KEYCORP REPORTS FIRST QUARTER 2022 NET INCOME OF $420 MILLION, OR $.45 PER DILUTED COMMON SHARE
text: Strong loan growth driven by consumer and commercial businesses Record loan originations from Laurel Road Net interest income reflects strong loan growth and liquidity deployment Noninterest income adversely impacted by market conditions late in the quarter Credit quality remains strong with net charge-offs to average loans of 13 basis points CLEVELAND , April 21, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- KeyCorp (NYSE: KEY) today announced net income from continuing operations attributable to Key common shareholders of $420 million, or $.45 per diluted common share for the first quarter of 2022. This compared to $601 million, or $.64 per diluted common share, for the fourth quarter of 2021 and $591 million, or $.61 per diluted common share, for the first quarter of 2021.

title: Analysts Estimate KeyCorp (KEY) to Report a Decline in Earnings: What to Look Out for
text: KeyCorp (KEY) doesn't possess the right combination of the two key ingredients for a likely earnings beat in its upcoming report. Get prepared with the key expectations.

title: Survey Reveals Connection Between Financial Savviness & Use of Digital Banking Tools
text: Access to digital tools empower Americans to take control of their finances CLEVELAND , April 7, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- After a year in which many consumers had to rely heavily on digital tools for their financial needs, self-reported "financial experts" use digital tools more often than others, according to the KeyBank 2022 Financial Mobility Survey —highlighting a potential correlation between savviness, confidence, and the usage of digital banking tools. Four in ten Americans say that access to digital banking tools make them feel empowered to take control of their finances, the highest-reported feeling among respondents.
